dapp-developer.title: Web and Blockchain Fundamentals
dapp-developer.description: Understand how the web works and how blockchain differs from traditional client-server architecture. Learn about decentralization, consensus, and why smart contracts exist.

resources.label: But How Does Bitcoin Actually Work?

dapp-developer.challenge: Write a plain-text explanation of how a transaction goes from a user wallet to a confirmed block on Ethereum.

dapp-developer.title: Solidity Basics
dapp-developer.description: Learn the Solidity language syntax, data types, and how the EVM executes bytecode. Write and deploy your first smart contracts using Remix IDE.
dapp-developer.challenge: Deploy a simple storage contract on Remix that stores and retrieves a number, then read the emitted event log.

dapp-developer.title: Ethereum Developer Toolchain
dapp-developer.description: Set up a professional local Ethereum development environment using Hardhat. Learn to compile, test, and deploy contracts from the command line.

resources.label: Full Stack Ethereum Development - Nader Dabit

dapp-developer.challenge: Create a Hardhat project, write a deploy script for a simple counter contract, and log the deployed address to console.

dapp-developer.title: Connecting React to Ethereum with ethers.js
dapp-developer.description: Build a React frontend that connects to MetaMask and reads data from a deployed smart contract using ethers.js. Understand providers, signers, and contract instances.

resources.label: Full Stack dApp Tutorial - Nader Dabit
Build a dApp with React and Solidity

dapp-developer.challenge: Build a React page that connects to MetaMask, reads a value from your counter contract, and shows the current block number.

dapp-developer.title: Smart Contract Patterns and ERC Standards
dapp-developer.description: Learn widely used smart contract design patterns and ERC token standards. Use OpenZeppelin contracts as audited base implementations.
dapp-developer.challenge: Deploy an ERC-20 token using OpenZeppelin, then write a React button that transfers tokens between two test accounts.

dapp-developer.title: Testing Smart Contracts
dapp-developer.description: Write comprehensive unit and integration tests for smart contracts using Hardhat and Chai. Understand how to simulate time, blocks, and edge cases.
dapp-developer.challenge: Achieve 100% line coverage on your ERC-20 contract, including testing transfer-fail and allowance edge cases.

dapp-developer.title: Testnets, IPFS, and Contract Verification
dapp-developer.description: Deploy contracts to a public testnet using an RPC provider and verify source code on Etherscan. Store off-chain data on IPFS using Pinata or web3.storage.
dapp-developer.challenge: Deploy a contract to Sepolia, verify it on Etherscan, then store a JSON metadata file on IPFS and log its CID.

dapp-developer.title: dApp UX - Transactions, Errors, and State
dapp-developer.description: Build production-quality dApp UX that handles pending transactions, user rejections, network errors, and chain switching. Display real-time feedback to users.
dapp-developer.challenge: Add full transaction lifecycle UI to your dApp: pending spinner, success message, and friendly error handling for rejected or failed transactions.

dapp-developer.title: Smart Contract Security Fundamentals
dapp-developer.description: Learn the most common smart contract vulnerabilities and how to prevent them. Run automated security scanners and read through audit reports to build a security mindset.
dapp-developer.challenge: Run Slither on your ERC-20 contract, fix every high and medium finding, then write a short threat model for a simple DEX.

dapp-developer.title: Mainnet Deployment and Production Readiness
dapp-developer.description: Deploy a fully tested and audited dApp to Ethereum mainnet using production-grade tooling. Set up monitoring, alerts, and a multisig for admin functions.
dapp-developer.challenge: Deploy your dApp to Ethereum mainnet (or a mainnet fork), set up a Tenderly alert for any failed transaction, and document your rollback plan.

web3-frontend.title: Web and Ethereum Fundamentals for Frontend Devs
web3-frontend.description: Understand the Ethereum JSON-RPC API and how browsers connect to wallets. Learn the difference between a provider, a signer, and a contract interface.
topics: JSON-RPC methods like eth_call and eth_sendTransaction
web3-frontend.challenge: Use window.ethereum directly in a plain HTML page to request accounts and display the connected address.

web3-frontend.title: viem - Low-Level Ethereum Client
web3-frontend.description: Learn viem as a type-safe, modern alternative to ethers.js for Ethereum interactions. Build public clients for reading and wallet clients for sending transactions.
web3-frontend.challenge: Use viem to read the total supply and name of a deployed ERC-20 token on a testnet, then display the values in a React component.

web3-frontend.title: wagmi - React Hooks for Ethereum
web3-frontend.description: Use wagmi hooks to manage wallet connections, account state, and contract reads in React applications. Understand how wagmi wraps viem under the hood.
web3-frontend.challenge: Build a React component that uses wagmi to display wallet balance, let the user connect and disconnect, and read a contract value.

web3-frontend.title: RainbowKit - Wallet Connection UI
web3-frontend.description: Integrate RainbowKit to provide a polished multi-wallet connect modal with minimal configuration. Customize themes, chains, and wallet lists to match your brand.
topics: Custom theme with lightTheme and darkTheme
web3-frontend.challenge: Replace your custom connect button with RainbowKit, apply a dark theme, and restrict the app to Ethereum mainnet and Sepolia only.

web3-frontend.title: Reading and Displaying On-Chain Data
web3-frontend.description: Build data-rich dashboards that read multiple contract values, handle loading states, and refresh on block updates. Understand caching and revalidation patterns.
topics: React Query cache and stale time
web3-frontend.challenge: Build a token dashboard that displays name, symbol, total supply, and the connected wallet balance using a single useReadContracts call.

web3-frontend.title: Writing Transactions and User Flows
web3-frontend.description: Implement complete write flows including form validation, transaction submission, confirmation waiting, and error handling. Build flows that feel reliable to users.
web3-frontend.challenge: Build an ERC-20 transfer form with approve, transfer, and full transaction state feedback including pending, success, and error messages.

web3-frontend.title: Multi-Chain and Network Handling
web3-frontend.description: Build apps that work seamlessly across multiple EVM-compatible chains. Handle network detection, chain switching prompts, and chain-specific contract addresses.
topics: Detecting wrong network and showing warnings
Testing against L2 networks like Arbitrum and Optimism
web3-frontend.challenge: Make your dApp work on Ethereum mainnet, Arbitrum, and Optimism with the correct contract address loaded per chain.

web3-frontend.title: Signatures, Auth, and Sign-In with Ethereum
web3-frontend.description: Implement Sign-In with Ethereum (EIP-4361) for gasless authentication. Understand message signing, nonce-based anti-replay protection, and server-side verification.
web3-frontend.challenge: Build a SIWE login page that issues a JWT after signature verification and shows a gated dashboard to authenticated users only.

web3-frontend.title: Performance, Accessibility, and Testing
web3-frontend.description: Write unit and integration tests for Web3 React components using mocked providers. Audit performance and accessibility of your dApp and fix common issues.
topics: Mock Service Worker for RPC calls
web3-frontend.challenge: Write tests for your connect button, a contract read component, and a transfer form using mocked wagmi hooks, achieving 80% coverage.

web3-frontend.title: Production Build, Security, and Deployment
web3-frontend.description: Harden and deploy your Web3 frontend to production with secure RPC key handling, Content Security Policy headers, and monitoring. Perform a security review of client-side code.
topics: Hiding RPC keys via proxy API routes
Front-end phishing and address spoofing mitigations
web3-frontend.challenge: Deploy your dApp to Vercel with RPC calls proxied through an API route, add strict CSP headers, and verify no API keys are exposed in the client bundle.

defi-developer.title: Web and Blockchain Fundamentals for DeFi
defi-developer.description: Build the conceptual foundation needed to understand decentralized finance protocols. Learn how Ethereum transactions, gas, and smart contract state enable financial primitives.
topics: Smart contract state and storage slots
defi-developer.challenge: Write a one-page summary explaining how an AMM pricing curve differs from a traditional order book exchange.

defi-developer.title: Solidity for DeFi - Core Patterns
defi-developer.description: Master Solidity patterns critical to DeFi: interfaces, low-level calls, and token handling. Understand how protocols interact with each other through shared interfaces.

resources.label: DeFi Solidity Patterns - Smart Contract Programmer

defi-developer.challenge: Write a Solidity contract that holds ERC-20 tokens and distributes them proportionally to depositors without using floating-point math.

defi-developer.title: Foundry - Professional Smart Contract Toolchain
defi-developer.description: Set up the Foundry toolkit and learn to write tests in Solidity rather than JavaScript. Use Anvil for local forks and Cast for command-line contract interaction.
topics: Writing Foundry tests with Test and assertEq
Using Cast to call and send transactions
defi-developer.challenge: Migrate an existing Hardhat test suite to Foundry and add a fuzz test that covers at least 10,000 random input combinations.

defi-developer.title: Building Automated Market Makers
defi-developer.description: Implement a simplified constant-product AMM from scratch to understand how Uniswap V2 pricing and liquidity work. Learn how LP tokens represent pool shares.

resources.label: Build Uniswap V1 from Scratch - Smart Contract Programmer

defi-developer.challenge: Build and test a minimal AMM that correctly calculates output amounts including a 0.3% fee, verified with at least 5 Foundry fuzz tests.

defi-developer.title: Price Oracles and External Data
defi-developer.description: Integrate price oracles into DeFi contracts for safe, manipulation-resistant on-chain pricing. Compare Chainlink push oracles vs Uniswap TWAP pull oracles.
topics: Choosing oracles for different use cases
defi-developer.challenge: Write a contract that reads a Chainlink price feed and reverts if the data is stale by more than 1 hour, tested on a mainnet Anvil fork.

defi-developer.title: Lending and Borrowing Protocols
defi-developer.description: Build a simplified lending protocol with collateral requirements, health factor tracking, and liquidation logic. Learn how Aave and Compound manage risk.

resources.label: Build a Lending Protocol - Smart Contract Programmer

defi-developer.challenge: Build a minimal lending contract where users deposit ETH as collateral and borrow an ERC-20, with a liquidation function that becomes available below 1.2 health factor.

defi-developer.title: Flash Loans
defi-developer.description: Understand flash loans as atomic, uncollateralized loans used for arbitrage, liquidations, and collateral swaps. Implement a flash loan receiver contract.
defi-developer.challenge: Write a flash loan receiver that borrows USDC from Aave, performs a simulated arbitrage swap, and repays with fee - tested on a mainnet Anvil fork.

defi-developer.title: Yield Strategies and Vaults
defi-developer.description: Implement ERC-4626 tokenized vault contracts that automate yield strategies. Learn how yield aggregators like Yearn compose multiple protocols.

resources.label: Build a Yield Vault - Smart Contract Programmer

defi-developer.challenge: Build an ERC-4626 vault that deposits underlying tokens into Compound and compounds accrued COMP rewards on each deposit.

defi-developer.title: DeFi Security and Attack Vectors
defi-developer.description: Study the most common DeFi-specific attack vectors including reentrancy, oracle manipulation, governance attacks, and sandwich attacks. Reproduce past exploits in a test environment.
topics: Reading and learning from public audit reports

resources.label: How to Secure Smart Contracts - Trail of Bits

defi-developer.challenge: Reproduce the reentrancy exploit from The DAO hack in a Foundry test, then patch it and show the patched version passes all tests.

defi-developer.title: Mainnet Deployment and Protocol Launch
defi-developer.description: Deploy a production DeFi protocol to Ethereum mainnet with full auditing, monitoring, and a timelocked governance multisig. Build the operational runbook for incident response.
topics: Gnosis Safe multisig for protocol admin
defi-developer.challenge: Deploy your lending protocol to a mainnet Anvil fork, run the full audit checklist, set up a Tenderly alert for health factor breaches, and write an incident response plan.

nft-developer.title: Web and Blockchain Basics for NFT Developers
nft-developer.description: Understand the basics of how blockchains work and what makes NFTs unique compared to fungible tokens. Learn about digital ownership, provenance, and on-chain vs off-chain data.
nft-developer.challenge: Write a plain description of how owning an NFT differs from owning a JPEG file on your hard drive, referencing on-chain ownership records.

nft-developer.title: Solidity and ERC-721 Standard
nft-developer.description: Study the ERC-721 standard interface and implement a basic NFT contract using OpenZeppelin. Understand the tokenId model, ownership mapping, and approval mechanics.
nft-developer.challenge: Deploy an ERC-721 contract on Remix that mints tokens with sequential IDs and stores a custom name for each token.

nft-developer.title: NFT Metadata and IPFS
nft-developer.description: Create and pin NFT metadata JSON files to IPFS and reference them in your contract via tokenURI. Understand the metadata standard used by OpenSea and other marketplaces.
topics: Uploading images to IPFS via Pinata
nft-developer.challenge: Upload 5 images and 5 metadata JSON files to IPFS, then deploy an NFT contract where each token correctly resolves to its metadata via tokenURI.

nft-developer.title: NFT Minting Mechanics
nft-developer.description: Build NFT minting contracts with supply caps, allowlists using Merkle proofs, and public sale phases. Implement per-wallet mint limits and reveal mechanics.
nft-developer.challenge: Build an NFT contract with a 1,000-token supply cap, an allowlist of 100 wallets using a Merkle root, and a 2-per-wallet public mint limit.

nft-developer.title: ERC-1155 Multi-Token Standard
nft-developer.description: Learn ERC-1155 for efficient multi-token contracts that support both fungible and non-fungible tokens in a single deployment. Build a game item contract as a practical example.
nft-developer.challenge: Build an ERC-1155 contract representing 3 types of game items with different max supplies, and batch mint a starter pack of all 3 to a test address.

nft-developer.title: NFT Royalties and Marketplace Integration
nft-developer.description: Implement on-chain royalty standards using ERC-2981 and understand how NFT marketplaces enforce or bypass royalties. Learn about creator fee policies across platforms.
topics: On-chain royalty vs off-chain policy debate
nft-developer.challenge: Add ERC-2981 to your NFT contract with a 5% royalty, write a test asserting the correct royalty amount for any sale price, and verify it on a testnet marketplace.

nft-developer.title: On-Chain Generative NFTs and SVG Art
nft-developer.description: Generate NFT artwork entirely on-chain using Solidity-produced SVGs encoded as base64 data URIs. Understand the tradeoffs between on-chain and off-chain storage for art.
nft-developer.challenge: Build a fully on-chain NFT that generates a unique SVG based on the tokenId, with at least 3 randomized visual attributes stored in the token URI.

nft-developer.title: NFT Frontend with React and ethers.js
nft-developer.description: Build a minting website that connects to MetaMask, displays collection info, and handles mint transactions with proper feedback. Fetch and render NFT metadata from IPFS.
nft-developer.challenge: Build a minting page that shows total minted vs supply, lets users mint 1-5 NFTs, and displays a gallery of their owned tokens fetched from the contract.

nft-developer.title: NFT Contract Security and Testing
nft-developer.description: Audit your NFT contracts for common vulnerabilities including reentrancy on mint, supply manipulation, and metadata override attacks. Write comprehensive test suites.
topics: Supply cap bypass via batch mint
Fuzz testing mint and transfer functions
nft-developer.challenge: Run Slither and a 50,000-run Foundry fuzz test on your minting contract, fix all findings, and document each vulnerability and its fix.

nft-developer.title: Mainnet Launch and Collection Operations
nft-developer.description: Launch an NFT collection on Ethereum mainnet with a verified contract, marketplace listings, and an admin multisig for revenue withdrawals. Set up post-launch monitoring.
topics: Gnosis Safe for team fund management
nft-developer.challenge: Deploy and verify your NFT contract on mainnet, set up the OpenSea collection page, and configure a Tenderly webhook alert for any mint that fails.

web3-fullstack.title: Web and Blockchain Fundamentals
web3-fullstack.description: Understand the full-stack architecture of a dApp, connecting smart contracts on-chain to a web frontend and an off-chain indexing layer. Learn why each layer exists.
topics: Role of smart contracts as backend
What an indexer does and why it is needed
Next.js vs plain React for dApps
IPFS and Arweave for off-chain storage

resources.label: Web3 Full Stack Overview - Nader Dabit

web3-fullstack.challenge: Draw a labeled architecture diagram for a full-stack dApp showing smart contract, frontend, indexer, and wallet interaction layers.

web3-fullstack.title: Solidity Smart Contract Development
web3-fullstack.description: Write production-quality Solidity contracts using OpenZeppelin base classes and best practices. Understand events as the primary data emission mechanism for indexers.
web3-fullstack.challenge: Write a marketplace contract that emits a structured event for every listing and sale, with all indexed fields needed for efficient querying.

web3-fullstack.title: Hardhat - Contract Testing and Deployment
web3-fullstack.description: Use Hardhat to write tests, run a local node, and deploy contracts to testnets. Build reusable deploy scripts that track deployed addresses per network.
topics: Deploying with Hardhat Ignition or scripts
web3-fullstack.challenge: Write a full Hardhat test suite for your marketplace contract covering all public functions and edge cases, then deploy to Sepolia.

web3-fullstack.title: Next.js Frontend with wagmi and RainbowKit
web3-fullstack.description: Build a Next.js dApp frontend with wagmi hooks for contract interaction and RainbowKit for wallet connection. Set up the provider tree correctly for SSR.

resources.label: Build a Full Stack dApp with Next.js

web3-fullstack.challenge: Build a Next.js page that lists marketplace items from the contract using useReadContract and shows the connected wallet's listed items.

web3-fullstack.title: The Graph - Subgraph Development
web3-fullstack.description: Build and deploy a subgraph that indexes events from your smart contract into a queryable GraphQL API. Write entity schemas and mapping handlers in AssemblyScript.
topics: Deploying to the hosted service or Subgraph Studio
web3-fullstack.challenge: Deploy a subgraph for your marketplace contract that indexes all listings and sales, then query the last 10 sales with GraphQL.

web3-fullstack.title: Querying Subgraphs from the Frontend
web3-fullstack.description: Integrate Apollo Client or urql into your Next.js app to query subgraph data. Build paginated, filterable lists of on-chain events rendered server-side.
topics: Combining subgraph data with live contract reads
web3-fullstack.challenge: Replace all direct contract reads in your listing page with Apollo Client queries to the subgraph, adding pagination for collections over 20 items.

web3-fullstack.title: File Storage - IPFS and Arweave
web3-fullstack.description: Build file upload flows that store images and metadata on IPFS or Arweave from the browser. Learn the permanence tradeoffs between pinning services and Arweave.
topics: Uploading files to IPFS via Pinata API
Arweave and Bundlr for permanent storage
Handling large files and upload progress
web3-fullstack.challenge: Build a Next.js upload page that pins an image and a metadata JSON to IPFS via Pinata and displays the resulting IPFS URL on success.

web3-fullstack.title: Authentication and User Profiles
web3-fullstack.description: Implement wallet-based authentication with Sign-In with Ethereum and store per-user settings off-chain in a database. Combine on-chain identity with off-chain preferences.
topics: Iron Session for JWT-free session cookies
Next.js API routes for auth endpoints
Combining SIWE identity with subgraph data
web3-fullstack.challenge: Add SIWE auth to your app, protect a profile page with a session check, and store a display name per wallet in a Postgres database.

web3-fullstack.title: Testing, Performance, and Security
web3-fullstack.description: Test the full stack from smart contracts to frontend with unit, integration, and end-to-end tests. Audit for smart contract vulnerabilities and frontend security issues.
topics: Playwright E2E tests for wallet flows
web3-fullstack.challenge: Write a Playwright test that connects a wallet, lists an item, and verifies it appears on the listings page using a Hardhat testnet.

web3-fullstack.title: Mainnet Launch and Production Operations
web3-fullstack.description: Launch your full-stack dApp to production with verified contracts, a deployed subgraph on The Graph Network, and a Next.js frontend on Vercel. Set up monitoring across all layers.
topics: Publishing subgraph to The Graph Network
Vercel production deployment with env vars

resources.label: Production Web3 Full Stack - Nader Dabit

web3-fullstack.challenge: Deploy all three layers to production - mainnet contract, The Graph Studio subgraph, and Vercel frontend - and document the full deployment process.

wallet-developer.title: Web and Cryptography Fundamentals
wallet-developer.description: Understand the cryptographic primitives that underpin Ethereum wallets including elliptic curve keys, hashing, and digital signatures. Learn how private keys control accounts.
topics: Mnemonics and HD wallets BIP-32 and BIP-39
wallet-developer.challenge: Using only ethers.js, generate a random mnemonic, derive the first 5 addresses from it, and verify by re-deriving from the same mnemonic.

wallet-developer.title: EIP-1193 - The Ethereum Provider API
wallet-developer.description: Understand the EIP-1193 specification that defines how web pages communicate with Ethereum wallets through a standardized provider interface. Build a minimal provider consumer.
topics: Listening to accountsChanged and chainChanged events
wallet-developer.challenge: Build a plain HTML page with no libraries that uses window.ethereum to connect, send ETH, and display account and chain changes in real time.

wallet-developer.title: Key Management and Secure Storage
wallet-developer.description: Learn how to securely generate, encrypt, and store private keys in browser and mobile environments. Understand keystore file formats and hardware wallet integration.
topics: Web Crypto API for browser-native crypto
Secure key storage patterns in browsers
Hardware wallet communication via U2F or HID
wallet-developer.challenge: Build a mini key manager that generates a wallet, encrypts the private key with a password using ethers.js keystore format, and decrypts it on demand.

wallet-developer.title: Transaction Construction and Signing
wallet-developer.description: Build the logic for constructing, signing, and broadcasting Ethereum transactions with proper nonce management, gas estimation, and EIP-1559 fee fields.
wallet-developer.challenge: Write a function that constructs and signs an EIP-1559 transaction offline, logs the raw hex, and then broadcasts it separately using a provider.

wallet-developer.title: WalletConnect Protocol
wallet-developer.description: Integrate WalletConnect v2 to allow users to connect mobile wallets to desktop dApps via QR code or deep link. Understand the relay server and session lifecycle.
topics: Pairing URI and QR code generation
wallet-developer.challenge: Build a React app that uses WalletConnect AppKit to connect a mobile wallet via QR code, send a test transaction, and display the confirmation.

wallet-developer.title: Message Signing - EIP-712 Typed Data
wallet-developer.description: Implement EIP-712 typed structured data signing for human-readable signing prompts. Build permit-style approvals and off-chain authorization flows.
topics: Signature recovery and verification in Solidity
wallet-developer.challenge: Implement an EIP-712 signed permit flow where a user signs off-chain approval and a relayer submits the transaction on their behalf.

wallet-developer.title: Account Abstraction - ERC-4337
wallet-developer.description: Learn ERC-4337 account abstraction to build smart contract wallets with social recovery, gas sponsorship, and batch transactions. Understand UserOperation and bundler flow.
wallet-developer.challenge: Deploy a simple ERC-4337 smart account on a testnet and send a UserOperation that is sponsored by a paymaster covering gas fees.

wallet-developer.title: Phishing Prevention and User Security
wallet-developer.description: Build wallet features that protect users from phishing, transaction simulation previews, and malicious contract interactions. Study common wallet attack vectors.
wallet-developer.challenge: Build a React component that simulates a transaction using Tenderly before presenting it to the user, displaying token changes and warnings for high-risk functions.

wallet-developer.title: Multi-Chain and Cross-Chain Wallet Support
wallet-developer.description: Extend your wallet to support multiple EVM-compatible chains and implement basic cross-chain asset bridging flows. Manage chain-specific RPCs and network switching.
wallet-developer.challenge: Extend your wallet UI to support Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Polygon simultaneously, displaying balances on all three chains with a single wallet connection.

wallet-developer.title: Production Wallet Security Audit and Launch
wallet-developer.description: Perform a security audit of your wallet covering key storage, signing flows, network communication, and phishing surface area. Apply mitigations and launch with a bug bounty.
topics: Content script and background page isolation
wallet-developer.challenge: Complete a full threat model for your wallet using STRIDE, fix all critical and high findings, document mitigations, and publish a responsible disclosure policy.

gamefi-developer.title: Web, Blockchain, and Game Design Fundamentals
gamefi-developer.description: Understand the GameFi model combining blockchain ownership with game mechanics. Learn how on-chain assets, play-to-earn economics, and NFT ownership work in games.

resources.label: Play-to-Earn Game Design - Blockchain Game Alliance

gamefi-developer.challenge: Design a simple GameFi concept with a token economy: define what players earn, how they spend, and what assets are NFTs vs regular game data.

gamefi-developer.title: Solidity Basics for Game Contracts
gamefi-developer.description: Learn Solidity fundamentals with a game focus, writing contracts for in-game tokens and character NFTs. Understand how game logic can live on-chain.
topics: ERC-721 for unique characters or items
Access control for game admin functions
gamefi-developer.challenge: Deploy an ERC-721 character contract with on-chain stats (level, attack, defense) and an ERC-20 reward token that the game admin can mint.

gamefi-developer.title: Unity and web3.js Integration
gamefi-developer.description: Connect a Unity WebGL game to Ethereum using web3.js and the MetaMask browser extension. Read and write contract data from within a running game session.
topics: Calling JavaScript from Unity with jslib
gamefi-developer.challenge: Build a Unity WebGL game that reads the player NFT stats from the contract and displays them on a HUD overlay inside the game.

gamefi-developer.title: NFT Game Assets - ERC-1155 and Metadata
gamefi-developer.description: Use ERC-1155 for efficient batch minting of game items, weapons, and consumables. Store item metadata on IPFS and render attributes inside the game.
gamefi-developer.challenge: Create an ERC-1155 game item contract with 5 item types, mint a starter pack for a test player, and render the item attributes in a Unity inventory screen.

gamefi-developer.title: Randomness and Loot Generation
gamefi-developer.description: Implement verifiable random number generation using Chainlink VRF for fair loot drops and procedural game content. Understand why on-chain randomness from blockhash is unsafe.
topics: Mapping random words to loot tables
VRF callback and game state update
gamefi-developer.challenge: Build a loot box contract that uses Chainlink VRF to determine which ERC-1155 item a player receives when they open a box.

gamefi-developer.title: Play-to-Earn Reward Mechanics
gamefi-developer.description: Design and implement smart contract reward systems where players earn tokens through gameplay. Build staking mechanics for NFT characters and anti-bot protections.

resources.label: Staking Contract Tutorial - Smart Contract Programmer

gamefi-developer.challenge: Build a staking contract where players stake NFT characters to earn ERC-20 tokens per block, with a 24-hour unstaking cooldown.

gamefi-developer.title: On-Chain Marketplace for Game Items
gamefi-developer.description: Build a peer-to-peer marketplace for game items where players list, buy, and trade ERC-1155 assets using the in-game ERC-20 token. Implement royalties for creators.
topics: ERC-2981 royalty on game item sales
gamefi-developer.challenge: Build a game item marketplace where players can list items for a fixed price in game token, and the contract takes a 2.5% fee on each sale.

gamefi-developer.title: Layer 2 for GameFi - Low Gas and Fast Finality
gamefi-developer.description: Deploy game contracts on a Layer 2 network to reduce gas costs for high-frequency game transactions. Implement asset bridging between mainnet and L2.
topics: Gas cost comparison L1 vs L2
L2 transaction finality and sequencer trust
gamefi-developer.challenge: Redeploy your game contracts to Polygon Mumbai, test all game flows, and implement a UI showing gas cost savings compared to Ethereum mainnet.

gamefi-developer.title: GameFi Security and Anti-Cheat
gamefi-developer.description: Audit GameFi contracts for exploits including reward manipulation, NFT duplication bugs, and oracle gaming. Implement server-signed score submission for anti-cheat.
topics: Sybil attack resistance with per-wallet limits
Flash loan attacks on staking rewards
gamefi-developer.challenge: Implement server-signed score submission so only scores approved by the game server can trigger token rewards, and write a Foundry test demonstrating replay protection.

gamefi-developer.title: Mainnet Launch and Live Game Operations
gamefi-developer.description: Launch your GameFi project to mainnet with verified contracts, a production Unity WebGL build, and operational tooling for monitoring player activity and protocol health.
topics: Tenderly alerts for reward contract events
gamefi-developer.challenge: Ship a playable GameFi demo on Polygon mainnet with at least one NFT asset, one earn mechanic, and a Tenderly alert monitoring the reward pool balance.

dao-developer.title: Web, Blockchain, and DAO Fundamentals
dao-developer.description: Understand what a DAO is, how on-chain governance works, and why token-weighted voting enables decentralized decision-making. Survey major DAO platforms and patterns.
topics: What is a DAO and why it exists
Major DAO platforms - Compound, Uniswap, MakerDAO
dao-developer.challenge: Compare the governance parameters of Compound, Uniswap, and one other DAO: quorum, proposal threshold, voting period, and timelock delay.

dao-developer.title: Governance Tokens - ERC-20 with Voting
dao-developer.description: Implement an ERC-20 governance token with vote delegation using OpenZeppelin ERC20Votes. Understand checkpointing and why vote snapshots prevent double voting.
topics: Governance token vs utility token tradeoffs
dao-developer.challenge: Deploy an ERC-20Votes token, test delegation between 3 accounts, and assert that getPastVotes returns correct historical balances at specific block numbers.

dao-developer.title: OpenZeppelin Governor Framework
dao-developer.description: Build a complete on-chain governance system using OpenZeppelin Governor with quorum, voting period, and a timelock controller for delayed execution.
topics: Proposal lifecycle - Pending, Active, Succeeded, Queued, Executed
Proposing and voting in Hardhat tests

resources.label: Build an On-Chain DAO - Patrick Collins

dao-developer.challenge: Deploy a Governor and TimelockController, write a full test that creates a proposal, passes it through voting, queues it, and executes a parameter change.

dao-developer.title: Snapshot - Off-Chain Voting
dao-developer.description: Integrate Snapshot for gasless off-chain voting using signed messages stored on IPFS. Understand when off-chain voting is appropriate and how to build a custom Snapshot strategy.
topics: How Snapshot stores votes on IPFS
Snapshot and on-chain execution via SafeSnap
dao-developer.challenge: Create a Snapshot space for a test DAO, configure a custom token strategy, and simulate a proposal vote using the Snapshot SDK.

dao-developer.title: DAO Treasury Management
dao-developer.description: Build and manage a DAO treasury contract that holds ETH and tokens, executes expenditures only through governance votes, and reports on allocations.
dao-developer.challenge: Set up a Gnosis Safe as a DAO treasury, configure a 3-of-5 multisig, and execute a test fund transfer via a passed Governor proposal.

dao-developer.title: Aragon Framework
dao-developer.description: Build a DAO using the Aragon OSx framework with modular plugins for voting, permissions, and treasury. Understand how Aragon separates DAO core from governance logic.
topics: Aragon App for no-code DAO management

resources.label: Build a DAO with Aragon - YouTube

dao-developer.challenge: Deploy an Aragon OSx DAO on a testnet with the TokenVoting plugin, create a proposal via SDK, and execute it through the governance flow.

dao-developer.title: DAO Frontend and Governance UI
dao-developer.description: Build a governance dashboard that shows proposals, voting power, delegation status, and treasury balances. Query on-chain governance data from a subgraph.
topics: Transaction feedback for vote and delegate
dao-developer.challenge: Build a governance dashboard showing all active proposals with vote counts, a delegate button, and a treasury balance panel, sourcing data from a subgraph.

dao-developer.title: Advanced Voting Mechanisms
dao-developer.description: Explore advanced voting mechanisms beyond token-weighted majority voting. Implement quadratic voting to reduce plutocracy and conviction voting for continuous preference expression.
dao-developer.challenge: Implement a quadratic voting contract where votes cost the square of the voting power spent, and write a test showing it reduces large-token-holder dominance.

dao-developer.title: DAO Security and Attack Vectors
dao-developer.description: Study governance-specific attack vectors including flash loan governance takeovers, proposal front-running, and timelock bypasses. Harden your DAO contracts against each.
topics: Slither and Foundry fuzzing for governance

resources.label: Governance Attack Case Studies - Rekt News
Secure DAO Governance - Trail of Bits

dao-developer.challenge: Write a Foundry test that demonstrates a flash loan governance attack on a vulnerable Governor, then patch the contract and prove the attack fails.

dao-developer.title: Mainnet DAO Launch and Operations
dao-developer.description: Launch your DAO on Ethereum mainnet with token distribution, governance parameters, treasury setup, and community onboarding. Establish operational workflows for proposals and execution.
dao-developer.challenge: Deploy your complete DAO to mainnet with token distribution to 5 test delegates, pass a real proposal through full governance flow, and write an operational runbook.

zk-developer.title: Web, Math, and ZK Fundamentals
zk-developer.description: Build the mathematical and conceptual foundation for zero-knowledge proofs. Understand finite fields, polynomial commitments, and the intuition behind what ZK proofs prove.

resources.label: Proofs, Arguments, and Zero Knowledge - Justin Thaler

zk-developer.challenge: Write a plain-language explanation of how a ZK-SNARK allows proving knowledge of a secret without revealing it, using an analogy of your choice.

zk-developer.title: Circom - Arithmetic Circuit Language
zk-developer.description: Learn Circom to express computation as arithmetic circuits with rank-1 constraint systems. Write your first circuits and compile them to witness computation artifacts.
zk-developer.challenge: Write a Circom circuit that proves knowledge of two numbers a and b such that a * b = c without revealing a or b, and generate a valid proof.

zk-developer.title: snarkjs - Groth16 Proof Generation
zk-developer.description: Use snarkjs to run a trusted setup, generate Groth16 proofs, and verify them both in JavaScript and on Ethereum. Understand the proving and verifying key structure.
topics: Creating proofs with snarkjs groth16 prove
zk-developer.challenge: Complete a full Groth16 workflow: compile a Circom circuit, run trusted setup, generate a proof, and export the Solidity verifier contract.

zk-developer.title: On-Chain Proof Verification with Solidity
zk-developer.description: Deploy a Groth16 verifier contract to Ethereum and build a Solidity contract that gates functionality behind successful proof verification.
zk-developer.challenge: Deploy a verifier contract and build an application contract that mints an ERC-20 token only when a valid ZK proof is submitted, preventing double minting via nullifiers.

zk-developer.title: Noir - Rust-Like ZK Language
zk-developer.description: Learn Noir as a developer-friendly language for writing ZK circuits with Rust-like syntax. Use Nargo to compile and test circuits and Barretenberg as the proving backend.
topics: Private and public inputs in Noir
zk-developer.challenge: Rewrite your multiplier circuit in Noir, write 3 Nargo unit tests, and generate a proof using the Barretenberg prover.

zk-developer.title: ZK Identity and Private Credentials
zk-developer.description: Build ZK identity systems where users prove membership in a set or possession of credentials without revealing which member they are. Study the Semaphore protocol.
zk-developer.challenge: Build an anonymous voting app using Semaphore where 5 registered members can each vote once without revealing their identity.

zk-developer.title: PLONK and Universal Trusted Setup
zk-developer.description: Understand PLONK as an improvement over Groth16 with a universal trusted setup. Generate PLONK proofs using snarkjs and compare proving time and verifier gas with Groth16.
topics: When to choose PLONK vs Groth16
zk-developer.challenge: Generate both a Groth16 and a PLONK proof for the same Circom circuit and compare proof size, generation time, and on-chain verification gas cost.

zk-developer.title: ZK Rollup Concepts and zkEVM
zk-developer.description: Study how ZK rollups use validity proofs to scale Ethereum. Deploy contracts to a zkEVM rollup and understand how EVM execution is proven with ZK-SNARKs.
topics: Validity proof vs fraud proof rollups
zkEVM types - bytecode compatible, language compatible

resources.label: An Incomplete Guide to Rollups - Vitalik Buterin

zk-developer.challenge: Deploy your ZK voting contract to Polygon zkEVM testnet, verify it works identically to the L1 version, and measure the cost difference.

zk-developer.title: Circuit Security and Soundness Testing
zk-developer.description: Audit ZK circuits for under-constrained signals, unused inputs, and soundness bugs that allow invalid proofs to pass. Use static analysis tools specific to ZK circuits.

resources.label: ZK Circuit Auditing - Trail of Bits

zk-developer.challenge: Run circomspect on your Semaphore circuit, fix all warnings, then manually attempt to forge a proof by submitting under-constrained witness values and confirm it fails.

zk-developer.title: Mainnet Deployment and ZK Application Launch
zk-developer.description: Deploy a production ZK application to Ethereum mainnet with a public trusted setup ceremony, verified on-chain verifier, and documentation for external proof generation.
topics: Organizing a public Powers of Tau ceremony
Mainnet verifier and application contract deployment
Proof generation SDK for end users

resources.label: Powers of Tau Ceremony Guide - Hermez

zk-developer.challenge: Deploy your ZK voting system to Ethereum mainnet with a verifiable trusted setup, publish a proof generation guide, and document the ceremony participant list.

web3-indexer.title: Web, Blockchain, and Data Fundamentals
web3-indexer.description: Understand why blockchain data requires specialized indexing and how smart contract events serve as structured data emission. Learn the basics of GraphQL as a query language.
topics: GraphQL vs REST for blockchain data
web3-indexer.challenge: Use the Ethereum JSON-RPC eth_getLogs method to manually fetch all Transfer events from an ERC-20 contract and display sender, receiver, and amount.

web3-indexer.title: GraphQL Schema Design for Blockchain Data
web3-indexer.description: Design well-structured GraphQL schemas for on-chain data with proper entity relationships, indexed fields, and derived fields. Learn schema best practices for The Graph.
topics: BigInt and Bytes types in The Graph
web3-indexer.challenge: Design a GraphQL schema for a DEX that models Token, Pool, Swap, Liquidity, and User entities with all necessary relationships.

web3-indexer.title: Building Your First Subgraph
web3-indexer.description: Create a subgraph from scratch using the Graph CLI, write AssemblyScript event handlers, and deploy to Subgraph Studio for testing. Index a simple ERC-20 or NFT contract.
topics: Entity store operations - save, load, remove

resources.label: Build a Subgraph - The Graph Academy

web3-indexer.challenge: Build and deploy a subgraph for an ERC-20 token that indexes all Transfer events and exposes a query for top 10 holders by balance.

web3-indexer.title: Advanced Subgraph Patterns
web3-indexer.description: Go beyond event handlers with call handlers, block handlers, and data source templates for factory contracts. Handle complex protocols with dynamic contract tracking.
topics: Call handlers for non-event contract calls
Data source templates for factory contracts
web3-indexer.challenge: Build a subgraph for a Uniswap V2 style factory that dynamically tracks each newly created pair using data source templates.

web3-indexer.title: Ponder - TypeScript-Native Indexing
web3-indexer.description: Use Ponder as a modern TypeScript-first alternative to The Graph for local and self-hosted indexing. Write type-safe event handlers with hot reload and a built-in GraphQL API.
web3-indexer.challenge: Index the same ERC-20 contract with Ponder, compare the developer experience and query latency vs your The Graph subgraph.

web3-indexer.title: Querying and Optimizing GraphQL APIs
web3-indexer.description: Write efficient GraphQL queries for large on-chain datasets with pagination, filtering, and ordering. Understand query complexity limits and caching strategies.
topics: Pagination with first, skip, and cursor
web3-indexer.challenge: Write 5 optimized GraphQL queries for your DEX subgraph: top pools by volume, recent swaps, user history, 24h price change, and TVL over time.

web3-indexer.title: Real-Time Data - Subscriptions and WebSockets
web3-indexer.description: Add real-time on-chain data to your frontend using GraphQL subscriptions and WebSocket providers. Subscribe to new events and update the UI instantly.
topics: Polling as a fallback for subscription-limited APIs
web3-indexer.challenge: Add a GraphQL subscription to your DEX dashboard so that new swaps appear live in a feed without page refresh.

web3-indexer.title: Multi-Chain Indexing
web3-indexer.description: Extend your indexing infrastructure to cover multiple EVM-compatible chains with chain-specific subgraphs and a unified query layer. Handle cross-chain data aggregation.
topics: Chain ID filtering in schema and queries
Separate vs unified subgraph for multi-chain
web3-indexer.challenge: Deploy your ERC-20 subgraph to both Ethereum mainnet and Arbitrum One, then write a combined query that aggregates total transfers across both chains.

web3-indexer.title: Subgraph Testing and Performance
web3-indexer.description: Write unit tests for subgraph handlers using Matchstick, profile slow queries, and optimize entity updates that touch hot code paths in high-volume protocols.
topics: Mocking events and contract calls in tests
Asserting entity field values in Matchstick
Reducing entity saves in hot handlers
Derived field vs stored field tradeoffs
web3-indexer.challenge: Write Matchstick tests covering all event handlers in your DEX subgraph with at least 90% handler coverage, and optimize the slowest query to under 200ms.

web3-indexer.title: Production Deployment on The Graph Network
web3-indexer.description: Publish your subgraph to The Graph decentralized network, add GRT signal to attract indexers, and set up monitoring and alerts for sync status and query errors.
topics: Publishing to The Graph Network vs hosted service
Monitoring sync status and indexing errors

resources.label: Deploying to The Graph Network - YouTube

web3-indexer.challenge: Publish your DEX subgraph to The Graph Network, add curation signal, confirm at least one indexer is serving it, and set up a dashboard monitoring query success rate.
